LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANTS JULIAN SUTTER, SYMBIO SYSTEMS, LLC, AND SYMBIO PRIME, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDICIAL ORDER TO EXPEL A MEMBER UNDER CAL. CORP. CODE 17706.02(E) IS DENIED. THIS MOTION IS COLLATERAL TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AND DEFENDANT DID NOT FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT. WHETHER PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS OWN SYMBIOSYSTEMS IN EQUAL PORTIONS IS A DISPUTED ISSUE THAT IS NECESSARY TO DETERMINE WHETHER EXPULSION IS PROPER. IT IS ALSO DISPUTED WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S $43,000 WITHDRAWAL WAS AN AUTHORIZED DISTRIBUTION OR CONVERSION. DEFENDANTS SEEK A REMEDY THAT IS INDEPENDENT OF THE ALLEGATIONS PLED IN PLAINTIFF'S ACTION. (SEE DONALD J. V. EVNA M. (1978) 81 CAL.APP.3D 929, 934.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judicial Order To Expel A Member Under Cal. Corp. Code 17706.02(E)
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rod Divelbiss of JRA Law Partners, LLP telephone number 415-788-4646 for Plaintiff.
James Hastings of JWRH telephone number 415-891-2015 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JULIAN SUTTER, SYMBIO SYSTEMS, LLC, AND SYMBIO PRIME, INC.'S Motion For Judicial Order To Expel A Member Under Cal. Corp. Code 17706.02(E) is denied. This motion is collateral to Plaintiff's claims and Defendant did not file a cross-complaint. Whether Plaintiff and Defendants own Symbiosystems in equal portions is a disputed issue that is necessary to determine whether expulsion is proper. It is also disputed whether Plaintiff's $43,000 withdrawal was an authorized distribution or conversion. Defendants seek a remedy that is independent of the allegations pled in Plaintiff's action. (See Donald J. v. Evna M. (1978) 81 Cal.App.3d 929, 934.) The prevailing party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Defendants Julian Sutter, Symbio Systems, Llc, Symbio Prime, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Litigation
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rod Divelbiss of JRA Law telephone number 415-962-2884 for Plaintiff.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Julian Sutter, Symbio Systems, LLC and Symbio Prime, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Litigation is denied. Defendant fails to establish that the parties ever entered into an agreement to arbitrate. Defendant's reply concedes that the cited operating agreement was merely a draft that the parties never executed because there was a dispute regarding control and resolving deadlocks. Plaintiff's declaration demonstrates that the parties never agreed upon the terms of the operating agreement, including the agreement to arbitrate. Defendant's cases are distinguishable and involve the situation where the LLC has a viable/enforceable operating agreement. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Default
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JULIAN SUTTER, SYMBIO SYSTEMS, LLC and SYMBIO PRIME, INC'S Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Default is granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b). The trial court has wide discretion to grant relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473. (Berman v. Klassman (1971) 17 Cal.App.3d 900, 909.) Defendants attempted to timely file their motion to compel arbitration and stay litigation. The court rejected the motion due to a clerical error. Defendants did not learn of the rejection and as a result of their mistake and excusable neglect, default judgment was taken against them. (Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b); James R. Hastings Declaration Exhibit E.) Thus, Defendants are entitled to relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b). Code of Civil Procedure section 1008 does not apply to this motion because it is an entirely separate motion from Defendants' August 27, 2015, Ex Parte nunc pro tunc Application. Although this motion is based on the same facts as the Ex Parte nunc pro tunc Application, the instant motion requests different relief and is based on a different legal theory. The prevailing party is to preapre a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)
